scenes.title: The Held City
scenes.biggestGap: The road and triage already work, but Hearthglen can still use more town-system texture so the place reads as a fortified Lordaeron hold under continuous reclearing rather than a generic disaster town.

sceneContract.placeAnchor: Hearthglen is a garrison town still holding behind walls, towers, chapel, stables, smithy yard, and triage lanes hacked out under siege pressure.
sceneContract.materialAnchor: Gate stone, stretcher boards, mud, burned hair, blankets, wood for pyres, and Uther's hammer keep the field physical.
sceneContract.povAnchor: Sylvanas reads the town as military infrastructure under strain first: lanes, reclearing tempo, bodies, and what kind of hold this really is.
sceneContract.dialogueAnchor: The refugee question and Uther exchange stay tied to stirrup leather, treatment tents, Light-work, and the movement between triage tasks.
sceneContract.exitAnchor: The command pavilion at the south wall must pull the scene out of aftermath and into order-space without losing the field's smoke and pressure.

scenes.title: The Tent
scenes.biggestGap: The briefing and source-plan debate risk turning into portable strategy dialogue unless map tools, canvas, relay strips, tack, and body-posture keep interrupting the room.

sceneContract.placeAnchor: The command pavilion and horse line must feel like one war machine turning over beside Hearthglen's south wall.
sceneContract.materialAnchor: Wet canvas, map vellum, chalk, daggers, gauntlets, hammer leather, tack chests, and stiff new reins keep the scene from floating.
sceneContract.povAnchor: Sylvanas tracks rank, speed, strain, and whether each person turns knowledge into action or into a different kind of danger.
sceneContract.dialogueAnchor: Long strategy runs need nearby action beats that reveal tactic, relation, or pressure rather than decorative motion.
sceneContract.exitAnchor: The departure rush and repeated farewell must stay rooted in quartermaster labor, route handling, and the lane forced open toward Stratholme.

scenes.title: The Head Start
scenes.biggestGap: The launch is hot, but the ride can still collapse into generic speed language unless the Stratholme road keeps reading like grain-country logistics ground built to feed a city.

sceneContract.placeAnchor: The road to Stratholme is not empty travel space; it is cart-built grain country with lanes, ditches, barns, and traffic logic feeding a threatened city.
sceneContract.materialAnchor: Horse necks, reins, bit foam, road ruts, dust, and the district packet keep the launch embodied.
sceneContract.povAnchor: Sylvanas reads the three-rider geometry as the chapter's real relational move and prices the road in terms of speed, exposure, and where trouble will first show.
sceneContract.dialogueAnchor: Any shouted planning on horseback needs to play against wind, rider position, and the road's infrastructure.
sceneContract.exitAnchor: The final beat should leave army dust behind them and a physically specific food-road ahead, not an abstract race.
